title: Don't Drive Me Away
content:
"Don't drive me away,
        But hear what I say:
        Bad men want the gold;
        They will steal it to-night,
        And you must take flight;
        So be quiet and busy and bold."

        "Slip away with me,
        And you will see
        What a wise little thing am I;
        For the road I show
        No man can know,
        Since it's up in the pathless sky."
meaning:
"Don't Drive Me Away" is a quintessential example of Louisa May Alcott's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
